faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Weehawken home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small irregular holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery; silky tubes or cases in closets and storage areas; adult moths flying around wardrobes; and visible larvae or webbing on fabrics. You may also notice damaged furs, felt, or other natural fiber materials. Our Weehawken inspection team can identify the specific moth species and extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth treatment in Weehawken?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of your property, number of affected rooms, and treatment method required. Larger Weehawken homes with multiple closets and storage areas, or properties with widespread infestations in carpets and upholstery, typically require more extensive treatment. We provide detailed estimates after our initial inspection.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take in Weehawken properties?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on property size and infestation severity. Most Weehawken homes require follow-up visits at 2-3 week intervals to target newly hatched larvae and ensure complete elimination. The full treatment cycle usually spans 6-8 weeks, with our technicians monitoring progress and adjusting treatments as needed for optimal results.

faqs.question: Can clothes moth treatments damage my furniture or belongings in Weehawken?
faqs.answer: Our treatments are specifically designed to be safe for fabrics, furniture, and stored items while effectively eliminating moths at all life stages. We use targeted applications, pheromone traps, and when necessary, heat or freezing treatments that won't harm your belongings. Our Weehawken technicians take extra care with valuable textiles, antiques, and delicate materials, and we provide guidance on pre-treatment preparation to protect your possessions.
